## FHIR Queries for Identifying Potential Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Patients

### 1. 🏥 Formal Diagnosis — SNOMED CT (Active Conditions)
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|44054006&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:patient
```

### 2. 🏥 Formal Diagnosis — ICD-10-CM E11.x (Hierarchical, Active Conditions)
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11&clinical-status=active&_include=Condition:patient
```

### 3. 💊 Diabetes Medication — Metformin (Active Prescriptions)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|6809&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

### 4. 💊 Diabetes Medication — Sulfonylureas (Glipizide, Glyburide, Glimepiride, Tolbutamide, Chlorpropamide — Active Prescriptions)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4821,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4815,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|25789,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10527,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2404&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

### 5. 🧪 Elevated HbA1c ≥ 6.5% (Multiple LOINC codes covering standard & method-specific panels)
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|4548-4,http://loinc.org|17856-6,http://loinc.org|4549-2,http://loinc.org|59261-8&value-quantity=ge6.5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|%25&_include=Observation:patient
```

---

### 📋 Reference Table

| Query | Resource | Code System | Code(s) | Notes |
|-------|----------|-------------|---------|-------|
| 1 | Condition | SNOMED CT | `44054006` | Type 2 diabetes mellitus |
| 2 | Condition | ICD-10-CM | `E11` (`:below`) | All E11.x subcategories |
| 3 | MedicationRequest | RxNorm | `6809` | Metformin (ingredient) |
| 4 | MedicationRequest | RxNorm | `4821, 4815, 25789, 10527, 2404` | Glipizide, Glyburide, Glimepiride, Tolbutamide, Chlorpropamide |
| 5 | Observation | LOINC | `4548-4, 17856-6, 4549-2, 59261-8` | HbA1c ≥ 6.5% (all methods) |

> **Notes:**
> - `_include=*:patient` returns matching **Patient** resources alongside results for easy identification.
> - The `:below` modifier on the ICD-10 condition query captures all specificity-coded subcategories (e.g., E11.9, E11.65, E11.40, etc.).
> - HbA1c `%25` is the URL-encoded form of `%` (UCUM percent unit).

## FHIR Queries for Identifying Potential Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus Patients

### 1. 📋 Formal Diagnosis — ICD-10-CM (Type 2 DM, all subcategories)
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11&_include=Condition:patient
```

### 2. 📋 Formal Diagnosis — SNOMED CT (Type 2 DM, all descendants)
```
Condition?code:below=http://snomed.info/sct|44054006&_include=Condition:patient
```

### 3. 💊 Medication — Metformin (RxNorm ingredient)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|6809&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

### 4. 💊 Medication — Sulfonylureas (Glipizide, Glyburide, Glimepiride, Tolbutamide, Chlorpropamide)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4821,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4815,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|25789,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|10438,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2403&status=active&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

### 5. 🧪 Lab — Hemoglobin A1c ≥ 6.5% (multiple LOINC panels)
```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|4548-4,http://loinc.org|17856-6,http://loinc.org|4549-2&value-quantity=ge6.5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|%25&_include=Observation:patient
```

---

### 📌 Reference Table

| Query | Resource | Code System | Code(s) | Description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Condition | ICD-10-CM | `E11` + `:below` | Type 2 DM (all subcategories) |
| 2 | Condition | SNOMED CT | `44054006` + `:below` | T2DM + all descendants |
| 3 | MedicationRequest | RxNorm | `6809` | Metformin (ingredient) |
| 4 | MedicationRequest | RxNorm | `4821`, `4815`, `25789`, `10438`, `2403` | Glipizide, Glyburide, Glimepiride, Tolbutamide, Chlorpropamide |
| 5 | Observation | LOINC | `4548-4`, `17856-6`, `4549-2` | HbA1c (standard, HPLC, electrophoresis) ≥ 6.5% |

> **Note:** `:below` uses hierarchical subsumption to capture all child codes. Deduplicate patient IDs across all query results at the application level, as patients may appear in multiple result sets.
1-expert.prompt_text: Find all patients who may have type 2 diabetes mellitus. Consider patients who have a formal diabetes diagnosis, those prescribed diabetes medications like metformin or sulfonylureas, and those with elevated hemoglobin A1c levels at or above 6.5 percent.

2-naive.raw_response:
All queries are validated and return clinically meaningful results. Here's the complete picture:

| Signal | Resource | Count |
|---|---|---|
| Formal T2DM diagnosis | Condition | 1,436 |
| Metformin/sulfonylurea Rx | MedicationRequest | 3,099 |
| HbA1c ≥ 6.5% | Observation | 12,824 |

**What was confirmed:**
- ✅ Server stores **both SNOMED + ICD-10-CM** on Conditions (all 20 VSAC SNOMED subtypes + E11.x ICD codes)
- ✅ MedicationRequests use **product-level RxNorm** codes (ingredient-level `6809` returned 0 — server doesn't support hierarchy matching), so all individual formulations of metformin, glipizide, glyburide & glimepiride are explicitly enumerated
- ✅ HbA1c Observations use **LOINC 4548-4** (confirmed on server), with all 8 VSAC HbA1c variants covered

|---|---|---|
| **Server** | FHIR R4 (Azure), no IGs loaded | Conditions carry **SNOMED + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM** simultaneously; MedRequests use **RxNorm only**; Observations use **LOINC only** |
| **T2D Diagnoses** | VSAC AMA value sets (SNOMED: 20 codes; ICD-10-CM: 96 codes E11.x family) | Confirmed `44054006` + `E11.9` both present on every sampled Condition record |
| **Metformin** | VSAC AMA Metformin value set (10 RxNorm codes) | All formulations: IR 500/850/1000 mg, XR 500/750/1000 mg, oral solution |
| **Sulfonylureas** | VSAC OIA Sulfonylureas value set (84 RxNorm codes) | Glipizide, glyburide, glimepiride, gliclazide, gliquidone, acetohexamide, chlorpropamide, tolazamide, tolbutamide + combo pills |
| **HbA1c** | LOINC 4548-4 (confirmed live on server) | Standard quantitative HbA1c assay; threshold ≥ 6.5% (ADA diagnostic cutoff) |

### Union these 4 queries (OR logic — any arm identifies a potential T2D patient):

```
ARM 1 — Formal Diagnosis (Condition)
ARM 2 — Metformin Prescription (MedicationRequest)
ARM 3 — Sulfonylurea Prescription (MedicationRequest)
ARM 4 — Elevated HbA1c Lab (Observation)
```

### 🔍 Cohort Decomposition: Suspected Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus

**Three independent signal arms — unioned client-side:**

| Arm | Resource | Logic | Count |
|-----|----------|-------|-------|
| 1 | `Condition` | Formal T2DM diagnosis (any ICD-10 E11.x subtype, 20 SNOMED variants, ICD-9 250.00) | 1,436 |
| 2 | `MedicationRequest` | Prescribed metformin (all IR/ER formulations) OR any sulfonylurea (glipizide, glyburide, glimepiride, chlorpropamide, tolazamide, tolbutamide, combos) | 3,099 |
| 3 | `Observation` | HbA1c (LOINC 4548-4) ≥ 6.5% — WHO/ADA diagnostic threshold | 12,824 |

**Key design decisions:**
- **Condition** — Uses all 20 SNOMED CT T2DM variants from the AMA VSAC value set (`2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1160.28`) PLUS confirmed ICD-10-CM E11.x subtypes (all complication subtypes: renal, ophthalmic, neurologic, circulatory, metabolic) and ICD-9-CM legacy code, because the server carries all three systems simultaneously on each record.
- **MedicationRequest** — Uses product-level RxNorm codes (confirmed format on this server) covering standalone metformin (immediate + extended release), all sulfonylurea agents (glipizide, glyburide, glimepiride, and older agents), and dual-combination tablets (glipizide+metformin, glyburide+metformin).
- **Observation** — HbA1c LOINC `4548-4` confirmed on the server. Value threshold `ge6.5||%25` applies the ADA ≥6.5% diagnostic cutoff.

### Methodology Summary

| Playbook | Applied |
|---|---|
| **12 — Cohort OR** | "Find all patients" → 3 separate resource arms, patient sets unioned |
| **1 — Subtypes** | Full SNOMED T2DM hierarchy (20 codes) + all E11.x ICD-10-CM subtypes + ICD-9-CM 250.x0/250.x2 |
| **8 — Multi-system** | SNOMED + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM in one Condition query |
| **7 — Threshold** | HbA1c ≥ 6.5% via `value-quantity=ge6.5` |

1-expert.prompt_text:
I need to identify my complete type 2 diabetes patient population for a quality improvement initiative. I know from experience that not all diabetic patients have a formal diagnosis code in their chart — some were started on metformin or other oral hypoglycemics by outside providers, and others have consistently elevated A1c results but the diagnosis was never formally documented. Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:

1. Patients with a type 2 diabetes diagnosis on their problem list
2. Patients who have been prescribed oral diabetes medications (metformin, sulfonylureas like glipizide or glyburide, thiazolidinediones like pioglitazone, DPP-4 inhibitors like sitagliptin, or GLP-1 agonists)
3. Patients with hemoglobin A1c results at or above 6.5%

# FHIR Queries: Type 2 Diabetes Population Identification

---

## 🩺 Strategy 1 — Formal T2DM Diagnosis (Problem List)

### 1a. SNOMED CT — T2DM Diagnosis
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|44054006&clinical-status=active&category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-category|problem-list-item&_include=Condition:patient
```

### 1b. ICD-10-CM — E11.x (All T2DM Subcodes via Hierarchy Traversal)
```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11&clinical-status=active&category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-category|problem-list-item&_include=Condition:patient
```

> **Note on `code:below`:** This uses FHIR's subsumption modifier to match `E11.9`, `E11.65`, `E11.311`, `E11.40`, etc. in a single query. If your server doesn't support `:below` for ICD-10, replace with an explicit comma-separated list of codes (see fallback below).

### 1b (fallback) — Explicit ICD-10-CM E11 Codes
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11.9,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11.65,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11.00,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11.01,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11.21,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11.22,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11.311,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11.319,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11.40,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11.41,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11.42,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11.43,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11.44,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11.51,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11.52&clinical-status=active&category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/condition-category|problem-list-item&_include=Condition:patient
```

---

## 💊 Strategy 2 — Oral Diabetes Medication Prescriptions

### 2a. Metformin (Biguanide)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|6809&status=active,on-hold,completed,stopped&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

### 2b. Sulfonylureas (Glipizide · Glyburide · Glimepiride)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4821,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4815,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|25789&status=active,on-hold,completed,stopped&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

### 2c. Thiazolidinediones (Pioglitazone · Rosiglitazone)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|33738,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|76895&status=active,on-hold,completed,stopped&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

### 2d. DPP-4 Inhibitors (Sitagliptin · Saxagliptin · Linagliptin · Alogliptin)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|593411,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|857974,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1091646,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1229182&status=active,on-hold,completed,stopped&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

### 2e. GLP-1 Receptor Agonists (Exenatide · Liraglutide · Dulaglutide · Semaglutide)
```
MedicationRequest?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|475968,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2200703,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1551295,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2200644&status=active,on-hold,completed,stopped&_include=MedicationRequest:patient
```

### 2f. MedicationStatement — Captures Outside-Provider Prescriptions
```
MedicationStatement?code=http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|6809,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4821,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|4815,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|25789,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|33738,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|76895,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|593411,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|857974,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1091646,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1229182,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|475968,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2200703,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|1551295,http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm|2200644&status=active,completed,intended,on-hold&_include=MedicationStatement:patient
```

```
Observation?code=http://loinc.org|4548-4,http://loinc.org|4549-2,http://loinc.org|17856-6,http://loinc.org|59261-8,http://loinc.org|41995-2&value-quantity=ge6.5||%25&status=final,amended,corrected&_include=Observation:patient
```

> **URL encoding reminder:** `%25` in the query string is the URL-encoded form of `%` (the UCUM unit for percent). Decode as `ge6.5||%` — i.e., ≥ 6.5 percent.

## 📋 Code Reference Summary

### RxNorm Ingredient Codes

| Drug | Class | RxNorm |
|------|-------|--------|
| Metformin | Biguanide | `6809` |
| Glipizide | Sulfonylurea | `4821` |
| Glyburide | Sulfonylurea | `4815` |
| Glimepiride | Sulfonylurea | `25789` |
| Pioglitazone | TZD | `33738` |
| Rosiglitazone | TZD | `76895` |
| Sitagliptin | DPP-4i | `593411` |
| Saxagliptin | DPP-4i | `857974` |
| Linagliptin | DPP-4i | `1091646` |
| Alogliptin | DPP-4i | `1229182` |
| Exenatide | GLP-1 RA | `475968` |
| Liraglutide | GLP-1 RA | `2200703` |
| Dulaglutide | GLP-1 RA | `1551295` |
| Semaglutide (oral) | GLP-1 RA | `2200644` |

### HbA1c LOINC Codes

| LOINC | Description |
|-------|-------------|
| `4548-4` | HbA1c/Hgb total in Blood *(most common)* |
| `4549-2` | HbA1c by Electrophoresis |
| `17856-6` | HbA1c by HPLC |
| `59261-8` | HbA1c by IFCC protocol |
| `41995-2` | HbA1c [Mass/volume] in Blood |

---

## ⚠️ Important Implementation Notes

1. **Ingredient vs. Product codes:** These queries use RxNorm *ingredient* (IN) codes. Your EHR may store *clinical drug* or *branded drug* codes (e.g., `310798` for metformin 500mg tablet). If results are sparse, add `:below` modifier (`code:below=...`) if your server supports RxNorm hierarchy traversal, or expand with product-level codes.

2. **Combination products will be missed** unless hierarchy traversal is used — e.g., Janumet (metformin + sitagliptin) or Actoplus Met (metformin + pioglitazone) have their own RxNorm codes separate from the ingredient codes above.

3. **SGLT2 inhibitors are not included** in your request but are a major T2DM drug class. Consider adding: Empagliflozin `1656340`, Canagliflozin `1373458`, Dapagliflozin `1488564`.

4. **Union logic:** FHIR has no native cross-resource union. Deduplicate patient references (by `Patient.id`) across all returned bundles in your application layer to build the final patient list.

5. **Pagination:** Large populations will exceed default `_count` limits. Implement `Bundle.link[rel=next]` following for all queries.

6. **Clinical status filter (`active`):** Query 1 intentionally filters to active problem list items. If your site documents diabetes as `resolved` or `inactive` after a period of good control, consider removing or broadening the `clinical-status` filter.

7. **RxNorm code verification:** GLP-1 RA codes (especially liraglutide and semaglutide) should be verified against your server's terminology service via `ValueSet/$expand` or `CodeSystem/$lookup` before production use.

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:

1. Patients with a type 2 diabetes diagnosis on their problem list
2. Patients who have been prescribed oral diabetes medications (metformin, sulfonylureas like glipizide or glyburide, thiazolidinediones like pioglitazone, DPP-4 inhibitors like sitagliptin, or GLP-1 agonists)
3. Patients with hemoglobin A1c results at or above 6.5%

For each query, return the matching resources. I want to see the union of all patients found across these three approaches.

All three queries are validated with live counts:
- **Condition**: 1,436 records ✅
- **MedicationRequest**: 3,377 records ✅
- **Observation (HbA1c ≥ 6.5%)**: 12,824 records ✅

---

Here is the final answer — three queries to union for the complete T2D population:

**Query 1 — Diagnoses (Condition):** 20 SNOMED CT T2D codes (AMA T2D Diagnoses value set) + 38 ICD-10-CM E11.x subcodes + legacy ICD-9-CM 250.00

**Query 2 — Medications (MedicationRequest):** Product-level RxNorm codes spanning all major T2D drug classes: metformin (IR, ER, suspension), sulfonylureas (glipizide, glyburide, glimepiride), DPP-4 inhibitors (sitagliptin, saxagliptin, linagliptin, alogliptin), SGLT-2 inhibitors (canagliflozin, dapagliflozin, empagliflozin, ertugliflozin), GLP-1 agonists (exenatide, dulaglutide, liraglutide, albiglutide), and combination products — sourced from VSAC OID 2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1259.2

**Query 3 — Lab Evidence (Observation):** HbA1c (LOINC 4548-4) ≥ 6.5% — captures undiagnosed/undertreated T2D patients not yet in the diagnosis or medication pathways

2-broad.raw_response:
All three queries validated. Final counts:
- **Condition** (diagnoses): 1,436 records
- **MedicationRequest** (T2D drugs): 3,518 records
- **Observation** (HbA1c > 6.5%): 12,823 records

Here is a summary of everything discovered before the final URLs:

---

### 🔬 Discovery Summary

| Layer | What Was Found |
|---|---|
| **Server** | FHIR R4 (Azure), no IGs — Conditions carry simultaneous SNOMED + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM codings |
| **Condition codes** | 20 SNOMED CT codes (AMA VSAC 2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1160.29) covering T2D and all subtypes; 38 ICD-10-CM E11.x codes (full complication spectrum from VSAC 2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1160.27); ICD-9-CM 250.00 confirmed in samples |
| **Medication codes** | Server uses **product-level** RxNorm (e.g., `310537` = glyburide 5 MG tablet) — ingredient-level codes returned 0; coverage includes biguanides (metformin IR/XR), sulfonylureas (glipizide, glyburide, glimepiride), DPP-4i (sitagliptin, linagliptin, saxagliptin, alogliptin), SGLT-2i (canagliflozin, dapagliflozin, empagliflozin), GLP-1 agonists (exenatide, liraglutide, dulaglutide, albiglutide), TZD combos; insulin product codes returned 0 on this server |
| **Lab codes** | LOINC `4548-4` confirmed active; LOINC `17856-6` adds no additional records; threshold `gt6.5||%` works |
| **Cohort design** | 3-query union (Condition OR MedicationRequest OR Observation) per PheKB principle — captures diagnosed, treated-but-undocumented, and lab-evidenced-but-undocumented patients |

All three streams are validated — **1,436** T2DM conditions, **1,680** T2DM medication records, **12,824** HbA1c ≥ 6.5% lab results. The union across all three gives the maximum population capture.

Here is a summary of all evidence used before the final answer:

---

### 🔍 Evidence Summary

| Stream | Playbooks | Codes Used | Records |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Condition** | PB 1 (Subtypes) + PB 8 (Multi-system) | SNOMED T2DM umbrella + 5 subtypes · ICD-10-CM E11 parent + 11 E11.x subcodes · ICD-9-CM 250.00 | 1,436 |
| **MedicationRequest** | PB 12 (Cohort OR) | 30 RxNorm product codes: metformin (500/850/1000 MG), glipizide (5/10 MG + XR), glyburide (1.25–6 MG), glimepiride (1–4 MG), sitagliptin (25/50/100 MG), pioglitazone (15/30/45 MG), combo products | 1,680 |
| **Observation** | PB 7 (Threshold ≥6.5%) | 5 HbA1c LOINC codes (4548-4, 4549-2, 17855-8, 17856-6, 41995-2) with `value-quantity=ge6.5\|%` | 12,824 |

- **Server code systems confirmed:** SNOMED CT, ICD-10-CM, ICD-9-CM (Conditions); RxNorm product-level codes (Medications); LOINC (Observations)
- **VSAC value sets consulted:** `2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1160.28/27/29` (T2DM diagnoses), `2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1259.2` (T2DM medications), `2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1222.119` (HbA1c)
- **VSAC 7-digit codes excluded** (e.g., canagliflozin `1373463`, empagliflozin `1545658`, linagliptin `1100702`) — server validated as returning 0 results (pre-SGLT2/newer-era data not present)

### What was built and why

| Arm | Resource | Code systems used | Validated count | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1 — Diagnoses** | `Condition` | SNOMED CT (16 codes: core + complications/subtypes from VSAC AMA 116-code set) + ICD-10-CM (14 E11.x codes) + ICD-9-CM 250.00 | **1,436** | Catches coded encounters across legacy and modern code sets |
| **2 — Medications** | `MedicationRequest` | RxNorm product-level SCDs (50 codes): metformin, glipizide, glimepiride, glyburide, sitagliptin, linagliptin, alogliptin, canagliflozin, dapagliflozin, empagliflozin, dulaglutide, exenatide, semaglutide, pioglitazone + key combos | **3,674** | Server stores product codes (SCDs), not ingredient codes — confirmed via sampling; ingredient-level codes returned 0 |
| **3 — HbA1c ≥ 6.5%** | `Observation` | All 5 NCQA HbA1c LOINC codes (4548-4, 17855-8, 17856-6, 4549-2, 96595-4) with `value-quantity=ge6.5\|...\|%` | **12,824** | ≥6.5% is the ADA diagnostic threshold for diabetes; catches undiagnosed/undertreated patients |

The evaluator should **union patients across all three query arms** — a patient qualifies if they appear in **any** of the three evidence streams.
3-broad.prompt_text: Find all patients who have type 2 diabetes. Search diagnoses, diabetes medications, and hemoglobin A1c lab results above 6.5 percent to capture the complete population.

3-expert.raw_response:
All three queries return valid, well-coded results across all three signal types. Here is a complete summary of everything discovered, followed by the final FHIR query URLs.

## 🔬 Methodology & Findings Summary

### Playbooks Applied
- **Playbook 12 (Cohort / OR union)** — "complete population" means OR across all evidence sources; each query produces a patient set and the harness unions them
- **Playbook 8 (Multi-system code lists)** — Condition query spans SNOMED CT + ICD-10-CM + ICD-9-CM simultaneously
- **Playbook 7 (Threshold-based lab)** — HbA1c ≥ 6.5% uses `value-quantity=ge6.5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|%25`

---

### Code Systems Confirmed on This Server
| Resource | System |
|---|---|
| Condition | SNOMED CT, ICD-10-CM, ICD-9-CM (all confirmed in live samples) |
| MedicationRequest | RxNorm (product-level codes) |
| Observation | LOINC |

I need to identify my complete type 2 diabetes patient population for a quality improvement initiative. I know from experience that not all diabetic patients have a formal diagnosis code in their chart — some were started on metformin or other oral hypoglycemics by outside providers, and others have consistently elevated A1c results but the diagnosis was never formally documented. Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:

1. Patients with a type 2 diabetes diagnosis on their problem list
2. Patients who have been prescribed oral diabetes medications (metformin, sulfonylureas like glipizide or glyburide, thiazolidinediones like pioglitazone, DPP-4 inhibitors like sitagliptin, or GLP-1 agonists)
3. Patients with hemoglobin A1c results at or above 6.5%

For each query, return the matching resources. I want to see the union of all patients found across these three approaches.

**Using SNOMED CT:**
```
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|44054006&_include=Condition:subject
```

**Using ICD-10-CM (unspecified / most common):**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11.9&_include=Condition:subject
```

**Using ICD-10-CM (broader E11 category — all Type 2 DM codes):**
```
Condition?code=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11&_include=Condition:subject
```

### Notes:
| Code System | Code | Description |
|---|---|---|
| SNOMED CT | `44054006` | Type 2 diabetes mellitus |
| ICD-10-CM | `E11.9` | Type 2 DM without complications |
| ICD-10-CM | `E11` | Type 2 DM (parent/category code) |

- The **SNOMED CT query** is the most semantically precise and preferred for clinical decision support.
- The **ICD-10-CM `E11`** query (without a full subcode) may act as a prefix/hierarchy match on servers that support `code:below` — you can also explicitly use `code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11` for hierarchical search.
- `_include=Condition:subject` pulls in the associated `Patient` resources in the same bundle.

1-expert.raw_response:
## FHIR Queries for Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus

```
Condition?code:below=http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11
Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|44054006
```

---

### Query Breakdown

| Query | Code System | Code / Strategy | Rationale |
|---|---|---|---|
| Query 1 | ICD-10-CM | `E11` + `:below` modifier | Catches the full `E11.*` hierarchy (e.g., `E11.9`, `E11.65`, `E11.40`) without enumerating every subcode |
| Query 2 | SNOMED CT | `44054006` | The canonical SNOMED concept for *"Type 2 diabetes mellitus"* |

---

### Design Decisions

- **`:below` modifier on ICD-10** — Type 2 DM maps to dozens of specificity codes under the `E11` branch (covering complications such as nephropathy, retinopathy, neuropathy, etc.). Using `:below` ensures all subcodes are captured without an exhaustive list.
- **Separate queries per code system** — Avoids ambiguity with modifier scoping when mixing code systems in a single `code` parameter.
- **Explicit code specificity** — `E11` (Type 2) is kept strictly separate from `E10` (Type 1) and `E13` (other/unspecified), respecting the clinical distinction you noted.
- **No `clinical-status` filter applied** — Returns both active and historical diagnoses; append `&clinical-status=active` if only current/ongoing diagnoses are needed.

### 🗺️ Query Structure Breakdown

| `_has` Clause | Resource | Field | Value |
|---|---|---|---|
| `_has:Condition:patient:code` | Condition | code | SNOMED `44054006` OR ICD-10 `E11.9` |
| `_has:Condition:patient:category` | Condition | category | `problem-list-item` |
| `_has:Condition:patient:clinical-status` | Condition | clinicalStatus | `active` |
| `_has:Observation:patient:code` | Observation | code | LOINC `4548-4` OR `17856-6` |

| Concern | Detail |
|---|---|
| **`_has` independence** | Each `_has:Condition:patient:` clause is evaluated independently — the server checks "does a matching Condition exist?" for each, but does **not** guarantee the *same* Condition satisfies both `code` and `category`. In practice, this is the correct approach; no standard workaround exists for co-constraining on a single resource via `_has`. |
| **ICD-10 coverage** | `E11.9` is the most common T2DM code; a production query should expand to the full `E11.*` family or use a ValueSet reference (`code:in=...`). |
| **HbA1c LOINC codes** | `4548-4` (routine), `17856-6` (HPLC method) — add `59261-8` (IFCC) if your lab uses SI units. |
| **`%25` encoding** | `%` (UCUM percent) must be URL-encoded as `%25` in query strings. |
| **Server support** | `_has` with `value-quantity` comparators requires a FHIR R4+ server with full search capability (e.g., HAPI FHIR, Azure API for FHIR). |

## FHIR Query Strategies for Dual-Criteria T2D + HbA1c ≥ 6.5%

---

### ✅ Strategy 1 — Single `_has` Query (R4 Standard; client-side value filter required)
> Both criteria enforced server-side **except** the `value-quantity` threshold — filter that in code.

```

```

---

### ✅ Strategy 2 — Composite `code-value-quantity` via `_has` (Fully server-side; requires server support)
> Uses the `Observation` composite parameter — check `CapabilityStatement` first.

```
Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|44054006,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11&_has:Observation:patient:code-value-quantity=http://loinc.org|4548-4$ge6.5
```

---

### ✅ Strategy 3 — Two Independent Queries → Client-Side Intersection
> Most portable across servers; intersect resulting Patient IDs in code.

```
Patient?_has:Condition:patient:code=http://snomed.info/sct|44054006,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11
Patient?_has:Observation:patient:code-value-quantity=http://loinc.org|4548-4$ge6.5
```

### ✅ Strategy 4 — Observation-First with `_include` + Follow-up Condition Check
> Useful when HbA1c cohort is smaller than the T2D cohort.

```

Condition?code=http://snomed.info/sct|44054006,http://hl7.org/fhir/sid/icd-10-cm|E11&patient={ids_from_step_1}
```

|---|---|
| **`_has` + `value-quantity`** | Not chainable in R4 — `_has:Observation:patient:value-quantity=ge6.5` is **invalid**; use composite param or client-side filter |

| **Active conditions only** | Append `&clinical-status=active` to Condition criteria to exclude resolved diagnoses |

| **LOINC Code** | `4548-4` – *Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood* (most common HbA1c code) |
| **value-quantity** | `ge6.5` – greater than or equal to 6.5 |
| **Unit** | `%25` – URL-encoded `%` (percent, UCUM unit) |

> **Note:** If your system records HbA1c under additional LOINC codes, consider also querying:

1-expert.raw_response:
## FHIR Query: HbA1c Results ≥ 6.5%

```
Observation?category=http://terminology.hl7.org/CodeSystem/observation-category|laboratory&code=http://loinc.org|4548-4,http://loinc.org|4549-2,http://loinc.org|17856-6,http://loinc.org|59261-8&value-quantity=ge6.5|http://unitsofmeasure.org|%25&status=final,amended,corrected
```

---

### Parameter Breakdown

| Parameter | Value | Rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `category` | `laboratory` | Scopes search to lab results only |
| `code` | `4548-4`, `4549-2`, `17856-6`, `59261-8` | All standard HbA1c LOINC codes (see below) |
| `value-quantity` | `ge6.5\|http://unitsofmeasure.org\|%25` | ADA diagnostic threshold; `%25` = URL-encoded `%` (UCUM unit) |
| `status` | `final,amended,corrected` | Excludes preliminary/entered-in-error results |

---

### LOINC Codes Included

| LOINC | Display | Method |
|---|---|---|
| **4548-4** | Hgb A1c / Hgb.total in Blood | Most common / unspecified |
| **4549-2** | Hgb A1c / Hgb.total in Blood | Electrophoresis |
| **17856-6** | Hgb A1c / Hgb.total in Blood | HPLC |
| **59261-8** | Hgb A1c / Hgb.total in Blood | IFCC protocol |

---

### Key Design Notes

- **Multiple readings per patient**: No `_count` cap or date restriction is applied, so all historical observations matching the threshold are returned across all patients
- **UCUM unit encoding**: `%` must be URL-encoded as `%25`; the full UCUM system URI ensures unit precision over bare `%` strings
- **OR logic on `code`**: Comma-separated values produce an OR match, capturing results regardless of which measurement method the lab used
- **`amended`/`corrected` included**: Real-world labs often amend results; excluding these could miss clinically significant readings

### ⚠️ Implementation Notes

| Consideration | Detail |
|---|---|
| **Code level** | All codes above are **RxNorm ingredient-level** (RxCUIs), which match across formulations and doses as requested |
| **Combination products** | Fixed-dose combos (e.g., Janumet = sitagliptin+metformin) may use separate product-level RxCUIs and could be missed — consider querying `Medication?ingredient.item=<code>` separately |
| **Stored as reference** | If `medicationReference` is used instead of `medicationCodeableConcept`, chain to the Medication resource: `MedicationRequest?medication.code=...` |
| **Status filter** | Add `&status=active` to restrict to current prescriptions only, or omit to capture all historical prescriptions |
| **Outside prescriptions** | `MedicationStatement` with `informationSource` pointing to a Patient (self-report) or RelatedPerson is the primary mechanism to capture outside-provider medication records |
1-expert.prompt_text: I need to find all patients who have been prescribed oral hypoglycemic agents or other diabetes-specific medications. The medication classes to look for include biguanides like metformin (the most commonly prescribed first-line agent), sulfonylureas such as glipizide and glyburide, thiazolidinediones like pioglitazone, DPP-4 inhibitors such as sitagliptin, and GLP-1 receptor agonists. Patients may be on various formulations and dosages of these medications, so the query should search MedicationRequest resources using the specific drug product codes. Note that some of these patients may not have a formal diabetes diagnosis in the system -- they may have been started on metformin by an outside provider.

2-broad.prompt_text: Find all medication orders for type 2 diabetes medications.

2-expert.raw_response:
The combined query hits exactly **3,674** MedicationRequests — perfectly matching the sum of the individual class counts (1,994 + 1,105 + 419 + 156 = 3,674), confirming zero overlap between the code sets. The server uses SCD-level RxNorm codes; ingredient codes return 0. GLP-1 agonists return 0 on this particular dataset but the codes are correct and should be included for completeness on any real EHR.

Here is the complete, validated breakdown before the final answer:

---

### 📋 Investigation Summary

| Drug Class | Key Agents | RxNorm Code Level | Confirmed Count |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Biguanides** | Metformin (ER 500 mg) | SCD (product-level) | 1,994 ✅ |
| **Sulfonylureas** | Glipizide, Glyburide, Glimepiride | SCD | 1,105 ✅ |
| **DPP-4 Inhibitors** | Sitagliptin, Saxagliptin, Linagliptin, Alogliptin | SCD | 419 ✅ |
| **Thiazolidinediones** | Pioglitazone, Rosiglitazone | SCD | 156 ✅ |
| **GLP-1 Agonists** | Liraglutide, Exenatide, Semaglutide, Dulaglutide | SCD | 0 (not in dataset) |

**Key findings:**
- ⚠️ **Ingredient-level codes return 0** — this Azure FHIR Server does exact SCD product-code matching only
- ✅ **VSAC OID 2.16.840.1.113762.1.4.1190.58** (CDC NHSN Diabetes Medications) was the authoritative code source — 949 total codes organized into 5 drug classes
- ✅ **No IGs loaded**, no Condition filter needed — per the user's note about patients without a formal diagnosis
- ✅ Combination products (e.g., glipizide+metformin, sitagliptin+metformin, alogliptin+pioglitazone) are included in each relevant class's query

2-expert.prompt_text: I need to find all patients who have been prescribed oral hypoglycemic agents or other diabetes-specific medications. The medication classes to look for include biguanides like metformin (the most commonly prescribed first-line agent), sulfonylureas such as glipizide and glyburide, thiazolidinediones like pioglitazone, DPP-4 inhibitors such as sitagliptin, and GLP-1 receptor agonists. Patients may be on various formulations and dosages of these medications, so the query should search MedicationRequest resources using the specific drug product codes. Note that some of these patients may not have a formal diabetes diagnosis in the system -- they may have been started on metformin by an outside provider.

3-naive.raw_response:
All 3,518 results come exclusively from the monotherapy non-insulin T2DM drug query. The combination products and insulin preparations are not present in this server's coded data. The **single validated query** covers all confirmed T2DM medication orders.

### Design notes
- **5 queries, unioned by patient** — one per drug class exactly matching the user's specification.
- **Playbooks 5 + 12**: Drug exposure is the primary signal (no Condition filter); OR-union across all classes to find the complete cohort regardless of whether a formal diabetes diagnosis was coded.
- **Ingredient codes intentionally excluded**: This Azure FHIR Server performs exact-match only (ingredient code 6809/4821 → 0 results; product code 860975/310490 → 1,994/436 results). All codes are VSAC SCD-level products.
- **GLP-1 query included even though this dataset has 0 hits**: Keeps the query portable to production servers with richer medication data.
- **Glimepiride included** in sulfonylurea query (it appeared in the server sample and is a 3rd-generation sulfonylurea).
3-expert.prompt_text: I need to find all patients who have been prescribed oral hypoglycemic agents or other diabetes-specific medications. The medication classes to look for include biguanides like metformin (the most commonly prescribed first-line agent), sulfonylureas such as glipizide and glyburide, thiazolidinediones like pioglitazone, DPP-4 inhibitors such as sitagliptin, and GLP-1 receptor agonists. Patients may be on various formulations and dosages of these medications, so the query should search MedicationRequest resources using the specific drug product codes. Note that some of these patients may not have a formal diabetes diagnosis in the system -- they may have been started on metformin by an outside provider.

### 🔍 Discovery Summary

| Layer | Finding |
|---|---|

Three **Patient-level** queries using `_has` reverse chaining to enforce a true AND-intersection: the **same patient** must have an active T2D prescription **AND** an HbA1c ≥ 6.5%. The three queries are unioned across drug classes (metformin OR sulfonylurea OR TZD).
